title: Easy Sudoku Backtracking Bug Fix: Unnecessary Backtrack

statement.markdown: Given a partially filled 9x9 Sudoku grid, implement a backtracking function to fill in the missing numbers. However, the function currently contains an unnecessary backtrack, causing it to fail. Fix the bug and provide a correct implementation.
statement.inputFormat: A 9x9 2D array representing the Sudoku grid, where 0 represents an empty cell.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the Sudoku grid is valid and a 9x9 2D array representing the filled grid.
statement.notes: The Sudoku grid is represented as a 2D array, where each cell is either 0 (empty), 1-9 (filled), or -1 (incorrect). The function should use backtracking to fill in the missing numbers.

examples.explanation: A correct implementation of the backtracking function to solve the Sudoku puzzle.

hints.text: Review the Sudoku grid constraints to ensure the function is using backtracking correctly.

editorial.title: Easy Sudoku Backtracking Bug Fix: Unnecessary Backtrack
editorial.intuition: The backtracking function should fill in the missing numbers and check for validity, rather than using an unnecessary backtrack.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Try all possible combinations of numbers to fill in the missing cells.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Review the Sudoku grid constraints and use backtracking to fill in the missing numbers.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0) { for (int k = 1; k <= 9; k++) { if (isValid(grid, i, j, k)) { grid[i][j] = k; if (solve(grid)) return true; } } grid[i][j] = 0; return false; } } } return true; } bool isValid(vector<vector<int>>& grid, int row, int col, int num) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { if (grid[row][i] == num || grid[i][col] == num) return false; } for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) { if (grid[3 * (row / 3) + i][3 * (col / 3) + j] == num) return false; } } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[][] grid) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0) { for (int k = 1; k <= 9; k++) { if (isValid(grid, i, j, k)) { grid[i][j] = k; if (solve(grid)) return true; } } grid[i][j] = 0; return false; } } } return true; } boolean isValid(int[][] grid, int row, int col, int num) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { if (grid[row][i] == num || grid[i][col] == num) return false; } for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) { if (grid[3 * (row / 3) + i][3 * (col / 3) + j] == num) return false; } } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> bool: for row in range(9): for col in range(9): if grid[row][col] == 0: for num in range(1, 10): if self.isValid(grid, row, col, num): grid[row][col] = num if self.solve(grid): return True grid[row][col] = 0 return False def isValid(self, grid, row, col, num): for i in range(9): if grid[row][i] == num or grid[i][col] == num: return False for i in range(3 * (row // 3), 3 * (row // 3) + 3): for j in range(3 * (col // 3), 3 * (col // 3) + 3): if grid[i][j] == num: return False return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class Solution { static solve(grid) { for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (let j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] === 0) { for (let k = 1; k <= 9; k++) { if (Solution.isValid(grid, i, j, k)) { grid[i][j] = k; if (Solution.solve(grid)) return true; } } grid[i][j] = 0; return false; } } } return true; } static isValid(grid, row, col, num) { for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) { if (grid[row][i] === num || grid[i][col] === num) return false; } for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { for (let j = 0; j < 3; j++) { if (grid[3 * (row / 3) + i][3 * (col / 3) + j] === num) return false; } } return true; } };

solutions.python:
class Solution: def solve(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> bool: for i in range(9): for j in range(9): if grid[i][j] == 0: for num in range(1, 10): if self.isValid(grid, i, j, num): grid[i][j] = num if self.solve(grid): return True grid[i][j] = 0 return False def isValid(self, grid, row, col, num): for i in range(9): if grid[row][i] == num or grid[i][col] == num: return False for i in range(3 * (row // 3), 3 * (row // 3) + 3): for j in range(3 * (col // 3), 3 * (col // 3) + 3): if grid[i][j] == num: return False return True

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
def main():
    t = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        grid = [[*map(int, input().split())] for _ in range(9)]
        sol = Solution()
        print('Yes' if sol.solve(grid) else 'No')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
